content:
# Water-Based Fluids

**Summary**: Drilling fluids that use water as the primary base instead of oil.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1994-shokoya|Shokoya, 1994]] evaluated the corrosivity of water-based drilling fluids in deep, hot wells.

- [[2012-attyah|Attyah, 2012]] evaluated WBF as a common drilling fluid in the Eastern Province.

- [[2017-alakbari|Alakbari, 2017]] evaluated properties of water-based-fluids using nanosilica and nanoclay additives.

## Scientific Context

- [[1994-shokoya|Shokoya, 1994]] Used in deep wells due to cost and environmental benefits compared to oil-based muds.

- [[2012-attyah|Attyah, 2012]] evaluated WBF as a common drilling fluid in the Eastern Province.

- [[2017-alakbari|Alakbari, 2017]] evaluated properties of water-based-fluids using nanosilica and nanoclay additives.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[1994-shokoya|Shokoya, 1994]] analyzed for chemical stability and corrosion potential.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Water-based fluids are less polluting and easier to manage than oil-based systems but present corrosion challenges. ([[1994-shokoya|Shokoya, 1994, p. 22]])


- 63.2% of the drilling companies used Water Based Fluids (WBF). ([[2012-attyah|Attyah, 2012, p. 10]])

- Not stated in source ([[2017-alakbari|Alakbari, 2017]])
